about.description1: Altruvex is a precision web engineering firm in Cairo. We build bilingual web systems for businesses that need architecture - not templates with a brand coat.
about.description2: Every project receives direct founder involvement. No outsourcing. No theme builders. No intermediary between the decision and the engineer.

founder.name: Ali Abdelhadi
founder.philosophy1: I am building Altruvex to become the reference standard in its category - a company recognized for engineering precision, structural clarity, and execution that holds under scrutiny.
founder.philosophy2: The goal is not to appear premium. It is to build work with sufficient quality and positioning that others can copy the surface - but not the standard behind it.

about.founderNote: You work directly with the founder - no account managers, no handoffs, no dilution between strategy and execution.

stat2.label: Recycled Code
stat2.sublabel: Custom-engineered systems from the ground up

stat3.label: Architecture-Driven Localization
stat3.sublabel: Multilingual engineered as a first-class citizen

about.title: One team.

bilingual.label: Symmetric Bilingualism
bilingual.sublabel: Multilingual share the same technical DNA

demo.ar_body: هذا نص توضيحي يظهر كيف يتكيف التصميم تماماً مع اتجاه الكتابة من اليمين إلى اليسار. كل عنصر يُعاد تصميمه، وليس مجرد انعكاس.
demo.en_body: This is example text showing how the design adapts completely to right-to-left direction. Every element is reconsidered, not just mirrored.
demo.title: Interactive Demo

bilingual.paragraphs:
Most sites treat Multilingual RTL as an afterthought - flip the layout and hope it holds. We design systems where direction is a first-class architectural decision from day one.

This is not about translation. It is cultural adaptation. Right-aligned navigation is not mirrored - it is reconsidered.

Every component, every layout, every interaction - built to perform in both directions without compromise.
bilingual.title: Structure that respects direction.

items.1: Accept projects with fixed deadlines imposed before the scope is understood
items.2: Build systems we cannot stand behind technically
items.3: Work through agencies that sit between us and the end client
items.5: Start execution before the problem is fully mapped

closing.description: Not every project needs this level of thinking. Some need speed. Some need iteration. But if you are building something that needs to last - if the system is the product, not just the storefront - we should talk. No pitch. No pressure. A direct technical discussion about what you are building and whether we are the right fit.
closing.title: If this matches how you think,

constraints.paragraphs:
Unlimited options create paralysis, not products. We begin every project by defining what we will not build - which features are out of scope, which use cases are excluded, which decisions are off the table.

This is not limitation. It is focus. A system designed for everyone serves no one well.

Constraints force decisions. Decisions create character. Character builds a product with a point of view.
constraints.title: Constraints are a design tool.

1.altruvex: Understand the problem users are actually trying to solve
1.common: Build the features users asked for

2.altruvex: Define architectural constraints before opening the editor

3.altruvex: Launch is when the system starts working - not when fixes start
3.common: We will fix it after launch

data.description: Before UI, before features, before brand - we map what the system needs to know. A weak data model cannot be rescued by clean code on top of it.
data.title: Data First

handoff.description: Code written for one person is fragile. Every commit assumes someone else will read and extend it six months from now.
handoff.title: Write for the Next Engineer

maintenance.description: A system that requires constant attention is not finished. We optimize for long stretches of stability where nothing breaks, nothing drifts.
maintenance.title: Minimize Intervention Cost

scale.description: Not because every project will reach it - but because growth assumptions change every architectural decision made today.
scale.title: Design for 10x

hero.description: Interface, architecture, copy, and CTA structure all emerge from one question: what must the buyer understand, and what should they do next?
hero.title: We do not design pages first.

card1.body: This site is built by us. Every animation, every Arabic line, every interaction - that is the production standard we bring to client systems.
card1.title: We show our work

card2.body: Fixed-scope Technical Audit at 15,000 EGP. Project investment ranges published. No scope creep disguised as discovery.
card2.title: We price transparently

card3.body: RTL is not a CSS override here. Our architecture treats Arabic as a first-class language - structurally equal to English from day one.
card3.title: Arabic is not an afterthought

authority.subtitle: Until client testimonials are earned, this is the proof you can inspect directly on the page in front of you.
authority.title: What you can verify right now.

altruvex-site.name: Altruvex.com - The Site Itself Had to Be the Proof
altruvex-site.outcome: Sub-1s mobile TTI, RTL switching in under 16ms with zero layout shift, and leads that arrive pre-qualified - with scope and investment already mapped before the first call begins.
altruvex-site.problem: In a market where agencies compete on price, Altruvex needed its own site to prove technical quality without borrowing enterprise credibility it had not yet earned.
altruvex-site.solution: We built a bilingual Multilingual site on a custom Next.js stack with native RTL architecture, performance-first frontend engineering, and a transparency flow that qualifies leads before the first conversation.
altruvex-site.summary: A bilingual Multilingual proof build designed to demonstrate technical quality, native RTL design, and lead qualification before the first call.

art-lighting-store.name: Premium lighting e-commerce
art-lighting-store.outcome: A production storefront with high-resolution product imagery, synchronized inventory, and a handoff the client team can operate without engineering support.
art-lighting-store.problem: The client sold high-end fixtures where material and finish detail matter, but their existing platform compressed images aggressively and loaded slowly. Customers could not properly inspect finishes or scale, and inventory was fragmented across multiple systems - creating overselling risk.
art-lighting-store.solution: We designed a custom Next.js storefront with an image pipeline tuned for high-resolution zoom without sacrificing load time. Inventory is synchronized via webhooks into a single source of truth, and pages are statically generated with targeted revalidation for fast, consistent delivery.
art-lighting-store.summary: High-resolution product imagery at scale with real-time inventory and fast page loads for a premium lighting retailer.

newlight-lighting-store.name: NewLight Lighting Store - Venturing into E-Commerce
newlight-lighting-store.outcome: NewLight's first online store launched on custom architecture with bilingual journeys, payments, and a catalog the team can manage day to day.
newlight-lighting-store.problem: As a leading lighting company, NewLight needed to transition their traditional showroom into a digital experience that allows customers to browse and buy easily while maintaining the brand's premium feel.
newlight-lighting-store.solution: We designed a custom e-commerce store focused on visual aesthetics. We built a flexible product management system and a structured checkout flow, with full bilingual support across all devices.
newlight-lighting-store.summary: When NewLight decided to launch their first online store, we built a custom e-commerce platform that matches their brand and supports bilingual buying on every device.

exclusions.label: Quoted Separately
exclusions.value: Branding from scratch, copywriting, paid stock, SMS, email credits, premium SaaS tools, and third-party subscriptions are not included unless listed in scope.

paymentTerms.label: Payment Terms
paymentTerms.value:
50% to start · 30% at milestone · 20% before launch
No deposit = no project start.

yearOne.label: Year 1 Setup
yearOne.value: Domain, SSL, deployment, and base hosting for Year 1 are included for standard website scopes. Higher-load infrastructure is quoted only when the project requires it.

pricing.description: The tiers below help buyers self-qualify quickly before opening full Transparency.
pricing.ownershipNote: Every project includes full source code ownership — not a Shopify subscription, not a WordPress plugin stack. You own everything, permanently.

01.a: We price against current custom-development market rates and the engineering complexity of the system. Architecture, workflows, integrations, and bilingual requirements drive the number - not arbitrary metrics like page count.
01.q: How do you determine the investment range?

02.a: No hidden fees. All project prices shown are before 14% VAT. Year 1 includes domain, SSL, deployment, and base hosting where relevant. Renewals, paid third-party tools, and channels like SMS are only billed if your scope actually uses them.

03.a: Milestone-based: a commencement deposit to secure the build window, then payments tied to discovery completion, design approval, and development delivery.
03.q: What does the payment schedule look like?

04.a: Yes. Every project is built on modular architecture so it can grow. Start with a Focused Website and layer on authentication, payments, or dashboards as the business evolves.
04.q: Can I upgrade my system tier later?

05.a: Yes. Our Focused Website tier is for companies that need custom work without jumping straight into product-level investment. For more complex systems, we phase the build so the investment matches the business stage.
05.q: Do you work with early-stage companies?

faq.subtitle: Common questions about our investment model and how we scope projects.
faq.title: Pricing Questions

pricing.label: 12 - Investment Logic

roi.calculation: A site rebuilt 18 months after launch because it could not handle integrations, user accounts, or traffic typically costs 2–3× the original build - plus the revenue lost during the gap.
roi.question: The real question is not what this costs to build. It is what the wrong build costs over two years.

timeSaved.label: Your Time Saved
timeSaved.sub: No hiring, managing, or code reviews

roi.title: The wrong system compounds in cost.

1.description: When you use our contact form or Transparency flow, we collect the following information:

2.description: We use the information we collect to:
2.item1: Respond to your inquiries and provide project investment estimates
2.item3: Improve our services and website experience
2.item4: Protect against spam and abuse (rate limiting, honeypot detection)
2.title: 2. How We Use Your Information

3.description: Your data is stored securely using industry-standard encryption. We use PostgreSQL databases with SSL encryption for all data in transit and at rest. We do not sell, trade, or transfer your personal information to third parties.
3.title: 3. Data Storage & Security

4.item1: Request access to your personal data
4.item2: Request correction or deletion of your data
4.item4: Lodge a complaint with a data protection authority
4.title: 4. Your Rights

body.p1: Our platform has run on a single Django application for six years. Deployment cycles are 48 minutes end-to-end.
body.p1_extra: because all squads deploy as one unit.
body.p1_hl1: Teams block each other on every release
body.p2_extra: We have 14 domain areas and just need the extraction sequence.
body.p3: All 11 teams write directly to the same
body.p3_extra: No service owns its data. We also have
body.p3_tracing: no distributed tracing across service boundaries.
body.p4_extra: but we cannot isolate which call chain is responsible. Budget is not the constraint - time is.

cta.body: Want us to run this analysis on your system?

brief.hook: We diagnose why your system is slowing you down - then fix it.

action.body: Run the extraction sequence only after tracing coverage and service ownership are in place. Start with low-dependency domains before high-coupling flows.
action.label: Action

connected.body: Shared database and missing tracing are the same root: no service owns its data, so none can be independently deployed.
connected.label: Connected

finding.body: Instrument first - you cannot safely extract a service whose failure modes you cannot trace. Then Strangler Fig: Auth → Inventory → Orders last.
finding.label: Finding → Action

question.body: How many cross-domain joins in the shared schema? That count determines extraction sequence per service.
question.label: Question

rflag.body: Premature decomposition creates a distributed monolith with identical coupling - 14 services, same problems.
rflag.label: Red flag

symptom.body: Release coupling, not microservices. Teams move at the speed of the slowest deploy - investigate topology before decomposition.
symptom.label: Symptom

brief.scrollHintDesktop: scroll to read through the engagement
brief.subtitle: Every annotation is a decision point. No recommendations before the debt is fully understood.
brief.title: The summary, read it

cta.description: Spend 30 minutes mapping your constraints. You will leave with a clear technical direction - no commitment required.
cta.title: Not sure which direction to build?

techStack.diagramLabel: Service architecture diagram - select a node to inspect dependencies
techStack.inspect: Select a technology node to inspect

nextjs.description: Full-stack React framework powering every project. App Router, server components, ISR, and edge runtime in one coherent system.

nodejs.description: Server runtime for API routes, background jobs, webhooks, and serverless functions. Non-blocking I/O at the core.

postgresql.description: Production-grade relational database. ACID compliant, with JSON column support, full-text search, and row-level security.

prisma.description: Type-safe database client - schema is the single source of truth, TypeScript types generated automatically. No raw SQL drift.

react.description: Component-based UI layer. Every interface is composable, reusable, and predictably state-driven with concurrent rendering.

tailwind.description: Utility-first CSS with design tokens baked in. Fast iteration, zero dead styles in production builds, and first-class dark mode.

typescript.description: Strict type safety across the entire codebase - APIs, database queries, and UI components all share a single type system.

techStack.subtitle: No trend-chasing. Every dependency earns its place in the architecture.
techStack.title: Deliberate Technology Choices

development.title: Portals & Product Builds

cta.description: We keep the system stable, secure, and performing so you can focus on scale - without being anchored by infrastructure overhead.
cta.title: Stability should not be a gamble.

maintenance.description: Dependable, proactive support for live systems that need regular updates, monitoring, and a technical partner who understands the codebase.

01.description: Issues detected and resolved before users encounter them - not after a support ticket arrives.
01.title: Proactive Monitoring

02.description: Dependency updates, CVE monitoring, and security hardening on a defined cycle - not when a breach forces the issue.
02.title: Security Patching

03.description: Regular Core Web Vitals checks and bundle analysis to prevent the gradual performance drift that quietly erodes conversion.
03.title: Performance Upkeep

04.description: Fast turnaround on copy changes, minor feature additions, and configuration updates - without opening a new project engagement.
04.title: Content & Feature Updates

05.description: Daily encrypted backups with a tested recovery process - not a checkbox, something that actually works when you need it.
05.title: Automated Backups

06.description: Direct access to the engineering team for critical issues - with response times that match the severity.
06.title: Priority Support Channel

addons.label: Third-Party Tools
addons.value: Optional Altruvex tools or subscriptions can be added when useful but are not bundled into every maintenance plan by default.

infra.label: Not Covered
infra.value: Domain renewals, hosting renewals, SMS, email credits, paid SaaS, and third-party subscriptions are billed separately from the monthly retainer.

scope.label: Scope Limit
scope.value: Maintenance plans cover system stability and small updates. Major features, rebuilds, or new page systems are scoped and priced separately.

vat.label: VAT

stats.body1: Maintenance is not reactive patching - it is the ongoing discipline that keeps a system performing the way it was built to perform.
stats.body2: We manage the technical layer so you can grow without being anchored by infrastructure that is quietly degrading.
stats.title: Stability is an engineering decision.

maintenance.subtitle: Focus on the business. We handle the system.

cta.description: We will review your current design, identify what is suppressing conversions, and map the right next step - in a free 30-minute call.
cta.title: Need an interface that actually converts?

webDesign.description: We translate your offer into sharper hierarchy, clearer messaging, and a direct conversion path so the site qualifies buyers before your team picks up the phone.

01.description: Every page structure is designed to guide the visitor toward a specific action - not just present information and hope they decide.
01.title: Conversion-Focused Layouts

02.description: We map user journeys before designing screens, so every interface decision is grounded in how people actually navigate.
02.title: User Flow Engineering

03.description: Typography, spacing, and contrast calibrated so the most important element always commands attention first.
03.title: Precise Visual Hierarchy

04.description: Design that extends your visual identity into a consistent system - not a snapshot that drifts six months after launch.
04.title: Identity System Alignment

05.description: No bloated assets or motion for its own sake. Every visual decision is measured by its impact on load time and Core Web Vitals.
05.title: Performance-Aware Design

06.description: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance built into the design system - not patched in after development closes.
06.title: Accessibility Compliance

deliverables: Responsive interface system for desktop and mobile
Multilingual page logic with true directional parity
Conversion-led layouts, CTA placement, and proof structure
fitItems: The current site looks acceptable but does not convert.
Your offer is strong but the hierarchy and message are unclear.
You need a custom website - not a template with brand paint.

webDesign.showcaseSubtitle: Every screen is a conversion opportunity, designed around your actual business outcomes.

serviceDetails.whatWeOfferSubtitle: Scoped to what your system needs - nothing more.

service1.description: React/Next.js systems built to onboard users, reduce churn, and generate revenue - not just to render on a screen.
service1.title: Custom Web Applications

service2.description: Custom storefronts engineered for speed and revenue. Every step from product page to payment optimized to reduce abandonment and raise order value.
service2.title: E-Commerce That Converts

service3.description: Arabic (RTL) and English (LTR) built as structural equals - not a translation layer added to an LTR base. Every component reconsidered for both directions.
service3.title: Bilingual Business Portals

service4.description: Your current system is slow, broken, or failing to convert. We audit, diagnose, and rebuild exactly what needs rebuilding - without a full restart.
service4.title: Performance Rescues

services.subtitle: Every layer engineered precisely - from the interface to the data model.
services.title: Four disciplines. One delivery standard.

servicesPage.title: Four focused disciplines.

accessibility.benchmarks: Lighthouse Accessibility > 95 | No ARIA errors | Contrast ratio > 4.5:1
accessibility.description: If it is not accessible, it is not finished. WCAG compliance is a build requirement - not a post-launch checklist item.
accessibility.requirements: Semantic HTML | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance | Keyboard accessibility | Screen reader support
accessibility.title: Accessible by Default

code.benchmarks: Zero lint errors | > 80% test coverage | Documented API endpoints
code.description: Code is read far more often than it is written. We optimize for clarity, consistency, and maintainability - not clever shortcuts that create future debt.
code.requirements: Strict TypeScript | Component-based architecture | Automated testing | Continuous integration
code.title: Clean, Maintainable Code

performance.description: Every 100ms of latency is a conversion leak. Performance is a design constraint from day one - not an optimization pass squeezed in post-launch.
performance.requirements: Edge-first delivery | Modern image formats | Minimal JS execution | Font optimization
performance.title: Speed as a Revenue Driver

security.benchmarks: Grade A security headers | No high-risk CVEs | Regular patch cycles
security.description: Trust is earned through security built in - not bolted on. CSP headers, dependency audits, and input sanitization before anything ships.
security.requirements: CSP headers | Secure authentication | Sanitized inputs | Dependency audits
security.title: Security from Day One

enforcement.description: CI/CD pipelines block deployments if these standards are not met. Quality does not degrade over time - it is enforced on every deploy.
enforcement.title: Accountability built in.

hero.description: Every system must be fast, readable, accessible, and maintainable enough that every decision after launch is easier - not harder.

webVitals.description: FCP < 1.5s · TTI < 3.5s · CLS < 0.1 on primary pages.
webVitals.label: Core Web Vitals

hero.title: Quality is a delivery requirement,

1.description: Altruvex provides web engineering, interface design, and related digital infrastructure services. All project scopes, deliverables, and timelines are agreed upon in individual project proposals.
1.title: 1. Services

2.description: Estimates provided through our online Transparency tool are preliminary and non-binding. This is an initial estimate to help you plan. Final investment and timelines are determined after a detailed requirements session and formalized in a signed project proposal.
2.title: 2. Project Estimates

3.description: Upon full payment, clients receive ownership of all custom-built deliverables as specified in the project agreement. Altruvex retains the right to use general methodologies, frameworks, and non-proprietary code patterns in future projects.
3.title: 3. Intellectual Property

4.description: Payment terms are outlined in individual project proposals. Standard terms include a commencement deposit upon project start with milestone-based payments tied to defined delivery phases.
4.title: 4. Payment Terms

5.description: Altruvex provides services on an "as-is" basis. We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from the use of our services or website.
5.title: 5. Limitation of Liability

6.description: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date.
6.title: 6. Modifications

7.description: For questions about these terms, please contact us at
7.title: 7. Contact

transparency.description: This is an initial estimate to help you plan. For a precise proposal tailored to your specific goals, Schedule a Briefing.

exitIntent.description: Most projects fail in the first conversation - not the build. A 30-minute call is enough to know if we are the right fit.
exitIntent.phoneError: Enter a valid phone or WhatsApp number.

exitIntent.subtitle: ONE LAST THING
exitIntent.successDescription: We will reach out on WhatsApp within 24 hours.
exitIntent.title: Before you leave.

faq.a1: Custom development gives you complete technical ownership, no platform constraints, and fundamentally better performance. A system built for your business logic loads faster, ranks higher, and converts more visitors than any plugin-dependent platform.
faq.a2: This is an initial estimate to help you plan. It is calibrated against current custom-development pricing and our delivery history. For a precise proposal tailored to your specific goals, Schedule a Briefing.
faq.a3: Yes. All projects include a 30-day post-launch warranty for critical fixes. Structured maintenance retainers are available for teams that need ongoing coverage, monitoring, and a predictable technical partner.
faq.a4: You receive your personalized estimate PDF immediately. We follow up within 24 hours to schedule a discovery call and walk through the requirements in detail.
faq.q1: Why custom engineering instead of WordPress or Shopify?
faq.q4: What happens after I request the PDF?
faq.subtitle: Common questions about our process and project investment model.
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions

pdfContent.estimateValid: This is an initial estimate to help you plan. For a precise proposal tailored to your specific goals, Schedule a Briefing. Preliminary estimate valid 30 days. 14% VAT excluded. Year 1 domain, SSL, and base hosting included where relevant; third-party subscriptions excluded unless scoped.
pdfContent.infraDetails: Domain registration (.com or equivalent) · SSL/TLS · deployment setup · and the standard hosting stack required for launch. Higher-load infrastructure, databases, SMS, email credits, and paid subscriptions are quoted separately only when the scope requires them.
pdfContent.whatsIncluded: What is included in your project

brand.hint: This helps us understand how much design groundwork is already in place.

complete.description: Full identity system ready: logo, colors, typography, and usage rules. We integrate it - no additional design scope needed.
complete.title: Complete

partial.description: You have a logo or basic visuals but no full system. We refine and extend what exists without starting from scratch.
partial.title: Partial

scratch.description: No brand identity yet. We build the full system - logo, colors, typography - as part of the project scope.
scratch.title: Starting Fresh

brand.title: What is your brand identity status?

budget.hint: Define your investment level - this calibrates scope, not ambition.

custom.range: 140,000+ EGP - enterprise architecture, multi-system builds, or phased technical programs.
custom.title: Architecture Consultation

large.range: 140,000+ EGP - custom products, dashboards, portals, and heavier operational systems.
large.title: Operational Product

medium.range: 70,000 – 140,000 EGP - connected systems with one or two serious integrations or richer bilingual scope.
medium.title: Connected Platform

small.range: 35,000 – 70,000 EGP - custom marketing sites and focused corporate builds.
small.title: Focused Website

budget.title: What is your allocated technical budget?

complexity.hint: This helps us calibrate scope, not just price.

basic.description: Core features only. Straightforward flows, minimal custom logic. The right choice for a disciplined first release.
basic.title: Focused

premium.description: Advanced logic, multiple roles, bespoke interactions. For flagship products and operationally complex systems.
premium.title: Full Depth

standard.description: Additional workflows, integrations, and richer UI. Everything in Focused, plus more depth where the business needs it.
standard.title: Standard

complexity.title: What scope level fits your needs?

content.hint: Content readiness directly affects both timeline and cost.

need-help.description: We co-create messaging and structure with you, which adds time to the early phases.
need-help.title: Need Content Help

provide.description: Copy and assets are structured and ready. We focus entirely on architecture, design, and engineering.
provide.title: Ready to Hand Over

unsure.description: Content is not yet structured. We clarify key messages and hierarchy before locking timelines and scope.
unsure.title: Not Defined Yet

content.title: How ready is your content?

deadline.hint: Your timeline shapes the team size and delivery strategy we recommend.

1month.description: ~4 weeks. We scope a focused, shippable version of the system for this window.
1month.title: Within 1 Month

2months.description: ~8 weeks from kickoff. Scope kept tight to protect quality without artificial pressure.
2months.title: Within 2 Months

flexible.description: 3+ months out. We optimize for depth, polish, and long-term maintainability.
flexible.title: Flexible

urgent.description: Under 4 weeks. Lean scope, high engineering intensity. Quality is non-negotiable regardless of timeline.
urgent.title: As Soon as Possible

website.description: A high-performance marketing site built for clarity, trust, and lead generation - no application logic.
website.title: Corporate Website

projectType.title: What are we building?

timeline.hint: Faster delivery requires more resources; flexible timelines reduce cost.

flexible.description: Extra time for iteration, testing, and refinement. Best for systems where durability matters more than speed.
flexible.title: Extended

standard.description: Structured cadence with enough time for discovery, design, build, and QA - no artificial pressure.
